topic.title: How to Install a Hyphenation Dictionary

indexterm.product: author developer editor authorEclipse developerEclipse editorEclipse

body.p: uses the TeX hyphenation dictionaries converted to XML by the OFFO project: .
The .xml files allow you to access the licensing terms and you can use them as a starting point to create customized dictionaries (see ).
The .hyp files are the compiled dictionaries that the actually uses.

The hyphenation dictionaries are located in: [CHEMISTRY_INSTALL_DIR]/config/hyph[OPE_INSTALL_DIR]/plugins/com.oxygenxml.pdf.css/lib/oxygen-pdf-chemistry/config/hyph[DITA_OT_DIR]/plugins/com.oxygenxml.pdf.css/lib/oxygen-pdf-chemistry/config/hyph.

One simple way to add more dictionaries:
Download and extract the offo-hyphenation-compiled.zip (or the offo-hyphenation.zip) file.
Copy the fop-hyph.jar file (containing the dictionaries bundle) to the [CHEMISTRY_INSTALL_DIR]/lib[OPE_INSTALL_DIR]/plugins/com.oxygenxml.pdf.css/lib/oxygen-pdf-chemistry/lib[DITA_OT_DIR]/plugins/com.oxygenxml.pdf.css/lib/oxygen-pdf-chemistry/lib directory.
Copy and extract the fop-hyph.jar file (containing the dictionaries bundle) into a directory in your project.
If you just need a single dictionary, copy the .hyp or .xml file extracted from the above jar.
Reference the directory using the args.hyph.dir parameter.
